coreJobStatement: Receive, store, and dispatch client goods with full inventory integrity
productJobStatement: Help Facility Safety & Security to maintain emergency exit door compliance and prevent unauthorized door use across large-footprint DC facilities operating under multi-shift schedules -- without mains wiring at each door point
lPath: L6 Facility Safety & Security (H) > L5 Emergency Exit Security Device

segments.name: High-throughput e-commerce DC
segments.characteristics: 24/7 multi-shift, high dock door frequency, large headcount
segments.targetabilityReason: Wired systems cost-justified; wireless fills secondary exits; highest door activation volume drives battery drain risk
segments.circumstance: 24/7 multi-shift, high dock door frequency, large headcount
segments.alternativesDiffer: Wired systems cost-justified at scale; wireless fills secondary exits

segments.name: Ambient bulk/pallet DC (3PL)
segments.characteristics: Large footprint, infrequent maintenance access, tenant variety
segments.targetabilityReason: Battery-powered wireless ideal; retrofit-first; highest volume segment for this product
segments.circumstance: Large footprint, infrequent maintenance access, tenant variety

segments.name: Cold-chain / regulated DC
segments.characteristics: Temperature-controlled zones, pharmaceutical/food compliance overlay, frequent audits
segments.targetabilityReason: IP-rated device required; stricter logging needed for compliance
segments.circumstance: Temperature-controlled zones, pharmaceutical/food compliance overlay, frequent audits
segments.alternativesDiffer: IP-rated device required; stricter compliance logging; battery performance affected by temperature

segments.name: Hazmat / restricted goods DC
segments.characteristics: Regulated goods (pharma, chemicals), mandatory tamper evidence
segments.targetabilityReason: Electronic exit control with log is mandatory; compliance pressure high
segments.circumstance: Regulated goods (pharma, chemicals), mandatory tamper evidence
segments.alternativesDiffer: Electronic exit control with log required by regulation

segments.name: Cross-docking terminal
segments.characteristics: High dock activity, many doors, shift-based staffing, no storage dwell
segments.targetabilityReason: Low cost per door; rapid deployment needed; highest door count per facility
segments.circumstance: High dock activity, many doors, shift-based staffing, no storage dwell
segments.alternativesDiffer: Low cost per door critical; rapid deployment needed

alternatives.name: Wired alarmed exit bars (e.g., Von Duprin 98/99)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Licensed electrician + conduit per door; high retrofit cost; reliable; centralized panel; no wireless flexibility

alternatives.name: Mechanical-only door alarm guard (e.g., GfS Basic, ABUS)
alternatives.tradeoffs: No remote monitoring; local alarm only; zero battery dependency for alarm; lowest cost; no event log

alternatives.name: Wireless electronic exit control (e.g., dormakaba SafeRoute, GEZE SecuLogic)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Higher per-door cost; richer BACnet/KNX protocol integration; battery-dependent for full function; EN 13637 compliant

alternatives.name: CCTV + staff patrol
alternatives.tradeoffs: High ongoing labor cost; no door-level alarm; GDPR complexity; no automatic compliance log; coverage gaps in non-camera zones

alternatives.name: Do nothing / accept risk
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost; full OSHA exposure ($16,550/violation serious, $165,514 willful/repeat); fire marshal citation risk; insurance premium impact

jobSteps.description: Determine which emergency exit doors require guarding, compliance threshold per zone, and wireless gateway coverage plan across DC footprint
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Product selection requires site survey to size gateway coverage and confirm which exits need EN 179/1125 hardware
jobSteps.rawStatement: Determine which emergency exit doors require guarding, compliance threshold per zone, and wireless gateway coverage plan across DC footprint
jobSteps.rationale: Product selection requires site survey to size gateway coverage and confirm which exits need EN 179/1125 hardware

jobSteps.description: Survey all emergency exit door locations; assess RF propagation constraints through steel racking, metal cladding, and reinforced concrete
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Wireless range constraint (C3) makes RF site survey mandatory before device placement; steel racking and metal cladding are primary attenuators in DC environments
jobSteps.rawStatement: Survey all emergency exit door locations; assess RF propagation constraints through steel racking, metal cladding, and reinforced concrete
jobSteps.rationale: Wireless range constraint (C3) makes RF site survey mandatory before device placement; steel racking and metal cladding are primary attenuators in DC environments

jobSteps.description: Install devices surface-mounted on door handles; deploy wireless gateways; commission the wireless network; test BMS integration
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Surface-mount retrofit without electrician is the primary installation differentiator for DC environments
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install devices surface-mounted on door handles; deploy wireless gateways; commission the wireless network; test BMS integration
jobSteps.rationale: Surface-mount retrofit without electrician is the primary installation differentiator for DC environments

jobSteps.description: Verify 95 dB alarm audibility in high-ambient-noise dock areas; confirm wireless link quality for all devices; complete commissioning checklist
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: DC ambient noise from IC/diesel forklifts (85-95 dB) and backup alarms (97-112 dB) can mask 95 dB alarm; wireless commissioning must be confirmed pre-handover
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify 95 dB alarm audibility in high-ambient-noise dock areas; confirm wireless link quality for all devices
jobSteps.rationale: DC ambient noise from IC/diesel forklifts (85-95 dB) and backup alarms (97-112 dB) can mask 95 dB alarm; wireless commissioning must be confirmed pre-handover

jobSteps.description: Mechanical guard physically prevents handle depression; 45-degree swing triggers 95 dB alarm and transmits alert to monitoring gateway
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Primary security function -- mechanical guard + alarm is the core value in warehouse environments
jobSteps.rawStatement: Mechanical guard physically prevents handle depression; 45-degree swing triggers 95 dB alarm and transmits alert to monitoring gateway
jobSteps.rationale: Primary security function -- mechanical guard + alarm is the core value in warehouse environments

jobSteps.description: Receive and act on real-time door status alerts across all monitored exits; track battery status remotely; escalate active alarms to shift operations team
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: IoT overlay is the key differentiator vs. mechanical-only; monitoring is the value proposition for 3-shift DC operations where patrol is impractical
jobSteps.rawStatement: Receive and act on real-time door status alerts across all monitored exits; track battery status remotely
jobSteps.rationale: IoT overlay is the key differentiator vs. mechanical-only; monitoring is the value proposition for 3-shift DC operations where patrol is impractical

jobSteps.description: Respond to alarm events; physically investigate the triggered door; reset device to secured state; log investigation outcome
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Post-alarm response is a real-time operational task; door identification and reset differ for authorized vs. unauthorized events
jobSteps.rawStatement: Respond to alarm events; physically investigate the triggered door; reset device to secured state; log investigation outcome
jobSteps.rationale: Post-alarm response is a real-time operational task; door identification and reset differ for authorized vs. unauthorized events

jobSteps.description: Generate compliance reports for fire marshal audit and OSHA 1910.36 inspection; execute battery replacement before depletion; archive event logs per regulatory retention requirements
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Bi-annual fire marshal inspections and OSHA 3-year NEP on warehouse safety make documented automated compliance logs a primary deliverable
jobSteps.rawStatement: Generate compliance reports for fire marshal audit and OSHA 1910.36 inspection; execute battery replacement before depletion
jobSteps.rationale: Bi-annual fire marshal inspections and OSHA 3-year NEP on warehouse safety make documented automated compliance logs a primary deliverable

stakeholders.who: Warehouse operator organization -- avoids compliance liability and incidents
Procurement / CFO -- CAPEX decision, ROI vs. basic mechanical or wired
Fire marshal, insurance underwriter, OSHA inspector

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of missing an emergency exit door requiring compliance coverage in a multi-building DC campus, resulting in a citation gap exceeding 1 door per 10,000 m2 floor area
Minimize the effort required to generate a site-specific wireless coverage plan for a DC facility with >30,000 m2 floor area and steel racking infrastructure
Minimize the likelihood of deploying wireless receivers in locations with insufficient RF signal penetration through steel racking, corrugated metal cladding, and reinforced concrete walls
Minimize the installation time per door for surface-mount emergency exit security retrofit without requiring licensed electrical contractors or structural modification
Minimize variability in installation quality across different EN 179 lever handle and EN 1125 panic bar configurations within the same DC facility
Minimize the likelihood that mounting configuration impairs single-motion escape capability, creating a non-compliance condition detectable only at fire marshal inspection
Minimize the likelihood that 95 dB alarm output falls below audibility threshold in dock areas with ambient noise levels of 85-95 dB from IC forklifts and backup alarms
Minimize the time to detect wireless device commissioning failures (dead zones, signal interference from MHE) before system handover to operations
Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized door-opening event going undetected due to battery depletion at a high-traffic dock exit during multi-shift operations
Minimize the false alarm rate caused by inadvertent handle contact by dock workers and forklift operators in narrow dock corridors, measured as false activations per 1,000 door passes
Maximize the number of emergency exit doors that can be monitored per wireless gateway installation across a DC facility exceeding 20,000 m2
Minimize the time between an emergency door-opening event and alert delivery to shift operations staff during 24/7 multi-shift DC operations
Minimize the effort required for operations staff to identify the precise physical location of the door that triggered an active alarm in a DC exceeding 20,000 m2 floor area
Minimize monitoring system downtime when a wireless gateway or receiver loses connectivity due to power outage or RF interference from warehouse equipment
Minimize the time to restore an emergency exit door to its secured state and return the monitoring system to active status following an authorized or unauthorized activation event

Increase the ability to distinguish between genuine unauthorized door use and inadvertent activation, with sufficient event context (timestamp, door ID, pre-event status) to support post-event investigation with >90% incident classification accuracy
Minimize the time required to compile emergency exit compliance evidence for bi-annual fire marshal inspection and OSHA 29 CFR 1910.36 audit, measured in staff-hours per inspection cycle
Minimize the likelihood of compliance documentation gaps caused by manual event log entry errors or missed recording during shift handover
Maximize the percentage of door events automatically captured in an audit-ready tamper-evident log without manual data entry by operations staff
Minimize the time to detect battery status degradation below the replacement threshold before alarm or monitoring capability is compromised, measured as lead time between alert and battery failure
Minimize the gap between actual door-event record completeness and an audit-ready compliance log, measured in preparation hours per inspection cycle
Minimize reliance on manually prepared paper or spreadsheet documentation during fire marshal and insurance audit
Minimize the maximum undetected duration of an emergency door status change during active warehouse operations
